title: Operational instructions and guidelines — Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Operational instructions and guidelines
This section contains policy, procedures and guidance used by IRCC staff. It is posted on the department’s website as a courtesy to stakeholders.
- Subscribe via RSS ( What is RSS ? )
- Updates to Program Delivery Instructions
Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) and Canada Border Services Agency employees consult operational bulletins (OBs) and manuals for guidance in the exercise of their functions and in applying the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act, the Citizenship Act and their Regulations.

## Program Delivery Instructions
- Temporary Resident Program
- Guidance that applies to many business lines: Standard requirements Language requirements Medical requirements Biometrics and identity management Fees collection Service delivery Interviews and interpreters
- Standard requirements Language requirements Medical requirements Biometrics and identity management Fees collection
- Language requirements
- Medical requirements
- Biometrics and identity management
- Service delivery Interviews and interpreters
- Interviews and interpreters
Operational manuals are divided into different categories. Each manual, in turn, is divided into specific chapters and assigned a chapter number.

Overseas Processing (OP)
Procedures Standard requirements for applicants: Application submissions Applications from diplomatic, consular and official personnel Service delivery: Extending the validity of visas Responding to requests for information under the Access to Information Act and Privacy Act Response to inquiries (migration offices): Clients and representatives Document retention/digitization and disposition Reconsideration after refusal Chapter 01 of the Procedures Manual (OP 01) has been updated and converted to program delivery instructions. Decommissioned date: 2022-xx-xx
Standard requirements for applicants:
- Application submissions
- Applications from diplomatic, consular and official personnel
- Extending the validity of visas
- Responding to requests for information under the Access to Information Act and Privacy Act
- Response to inquiries (migration offices): Clients and representatives
- Document retention/digitization and disposition
- Reconsideration after refusal
Chapter 01 of the Procedures Manual (OP 01) has been updated and converted to program delivery instructions.
Decommissioned date: 2022-xx-xx

Overseas Processing (OP)
Entrepreneur and Self-Employed (PDF, 488.13 KB) All applications in the self-employed persons class received on or before May 2, 2017, must be processed using the instructions from OP8 (PDF, 489 KB) . Processing offices should give applicants the opportunity to update their application to meet the requirements of OP8. All applications in the self-employed persons class received after May 2, 2017, must be processed under the SE PDIs , and officers should no longer use OP8 for SE processing.
Entrepreneur and Self-Employed (PDF, 488.13 KB)
All applications in the self-employed persons class received on or before May 2, 2017, must be processed using the instructions from OP8 (PDF, 489 KB) . Processing offices should give applicants the opportunity to update their application to meet the requirements of OP8.
All applications in the self-employed persons class received after May 2, 2017, must be processed under the SE PDIs , and officers should no longer use OP8 for SE processing.

### Operational bulletins
In exceptional circumstances, OBs may be issued for one-time-only instructions or to provide urgent instructions to staff for a brief period while the operational manuals are being updated.
